sidebar_position: 6
title: Code Generation
description: When and how to regenerate build_runner output after model changes
---

# Code Generation

The `core` package uses `json_serializable` and `build_runner` to generate JSON serialization code. If you change any model class in `core/`, you must regenerate before the change is usable by `app` or `designer_v2`.

## Why generated files are committed to git

Normally, generated `*.g.dart` files are excluded from version control. StudyU commits them because `core` is a path dependency imported by the other packages — those packages need the generated files to be present at import time without running code generation themselves.

## Regenerate after model changes

```bash
melos run generate
```

This runs `dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs` in the `core` and `designer_v2` packages. Stage and commit the resulting `*.g.dart` changes alongside your model changes.

:::warning
If you edit a model in `core/` and run the apps without regenerating, you will get compile errors in `app` and `designer_v2` about missing or mismatched serialization methods.
:::

## When to regenerate

You must run `melos run generate` whenever you:

- Add a new field to a model class in `core/`
- Change the type of an existing field
- Add or modify a `@JsonKey` annotation
- Create a new model class with `@JsonSerializable`
- Modify an enum that is used in a model

You do **not** need to regenerate for:
- Changes to non-model Dart files
- UI changes in `app/` or `designer_v2/`
- Configuration changes in `.env` files

## After pulling from remote

If someone else changed a model and committed updated `*.g.dart` files, you may get conflicts after pulling. If the generated files conflict with uncommitted local changes:

```bash
git stash
melos run generate
git stash pop
```
